introSection: Zoomerang is a Vekoma roller coaster currently operating at Lake Compounce in Bristol, Connecticut, USA. The ride officially opened on June 27, 1997.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Zoomerang has purple track and ocean blue supports.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: When the coaster starts, the train is pulled backwards up the lift hill, then dropped through the loading gate through a cobra roll and then a vertical loop. At the end of this cycle the train is pulled up the lift hill at the end of the track, then dropped once again allowing the train to go back through the loops backwards.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: In June 2001, a faulty sensor led to the brakes not engaging as the train passed backwards through the station. This allowed the train to enter the cobra roll, becoming stuck in the middle of the element. It took 90 minutes to evacuate all riders. A platform was subsequently installed in this location to ease evacuation should the train get stuck there again.

fullText:
Zoomerang is a Vekoma roller coaster currently operating at Lake Compounce in Bristol, Connecticut, USA. The ride officially opened on June 27, 1997.



Zoomerang has purple track and ocean blue supports.

When the coaster starts, the train is pulled backwards up the lift hill, then dropped through the loading gate through a cobra roll and then a vertical loop. At the end of this cycle the train is pulled up the lift hill at the end of the track, then dropped once again allowing the train to go back through the loops backwards.



In June 2001, a faulty sensor led to the brakes not engaging as the train passed backwards through the station. This allowed the train to enter the cobra roll, becoming stuck in the middle of the element. It took 90 minutes to evacuate all riders. A platform was subsequently installed in this location to ease evacuation should the train get stuck there again.
